title: Structurally-colored articles and methods for making and using structurally-colored articles
abstract: As described above, one or more aspects of the present disclosure provide articles having structural color, and methods of making articles having structural color. The articles incorporate a primer layer having a percent transmittance of about 40% or less in conjuction with an optical element. The optical element and primer layer impart a structural color to the article.
claims: Claims CLAIMS  We claim: 1. A method, comprising: disposing a primer layer having a percent transmittance of about 40 percent or less; and disposing an optical element on a top surface of the primer layer, wherein the primer layer, and the optical element impart a structural color. 2. The method of claim 1 , wherein the primer layer further comprises a textured surface, and the textured surface of the primer layer, the primer layer, and the optical element impart the structural color. 3. The method of claims 1 or 2, wherein the primer layer is on a top surface of a substrate of an article. 4. The method of any previous claim, wherein the top surface of a substrate is a textured surface of an article, wherein the primer layer is on the textured surface, and the textured surface of the substrate, the primer layer, and the optical element impart the structural color. 5. The method of claims 1 or 2, wherein disposing the primer layer comprising disposing the primer layer using: digital printing, offset printing, pad printing, screen printing, flexographic printing, or heat transfer printing. 6. The method of claim 5, wherein the primer layer is a metal oxide or metal oxynitride, wherein the metal oxide or the metal oxynitride is doped. 7. The method of claim 1 , wherein the primer layer is a coating, wherein the coating is a crosslinked coating including a matrix of crosslinked polymers. 8. The method of claim 7, wherein the matrix of crosslinked polymers include polyurethane polymers, wherein the polyurethane polymers include polyester polyurethane copolymers. 9. The method of any of claims 1 to 8, wherein the optical element is a multilayer reflector or a multilayer filter. 10. The method of any of claims 1-9, wherein the primer layer is substantially free of pigments, dyes, and a combination thereof. 11. The method of any of claims 1-10, wherein the imparted structural color is iridescent. 12. The method of any of claims 1-1 1 , wherein the optical element, as disposed onto an article, when measured according to the CIE 1976 color space under a given illumination condition at three observation angles between -15 degrees and +60 degrees, has a first color measurement at a first angle of observation having coordinates * and ai* and bi*, and a second color measurement at a second angle of observation having coordinates l_2* and a2* and b2*, and a third color measurement at a third angle of observation having coordinates l_3* and a3* and b3*, wherein the *, l_2*, and l_3* values may be the same or different, wherein the ai*, a2*, and a3* coordinate values may be the same or different, wherein the bi*, b2*, and b3* coordinate values may be the same or different, and wherein the range of the combined ai*, a2* and a3* values is less than about 40% of the overall scale of possible a* values. 13. A article comprising: a product of the method of any one of claims 1-12. 14. A article comprising: a primer layer having a percent transmittance of about 40 percent or less and an optical element, wherein the primer layer, and the optical element impart the structural color. 15. The article of claim 14, wherein the primer layer is on a top surface of a substrate of an article, wherein the optical element is on the primer layer of the substrate. 16. The article of any of claims 14-15, wherein the primer layer is between a textured surface and the optical element. 17. The article of any of claims 14-15, wherein the textured surface is between the primer layer and the optical element. 18. The article of any of claims 14-17, wherein the article is a yarn. 19. The article of any of claims 14-18, wherein the article is an article of footwear. 20. The article of any of claims 14-18, wherein the article is a sole of an article of footwear.
